title: Missing Quota Support in rootless podman
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I can't find any hint in the manual that the rootless podman does not support disk-quota or container file-size limitations (be it by missing implementation or privilege limitations of the running system).
Thus I guess it should be possible, but struggle to get it working

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
**Attempt No 1: Try to use an xfs backed overlayfs:**

1.1. configure the user storage config to use a size-limitation

```
cat <<EOF > ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options.overlay]
size="1024"
EOF
```
1.2. Set up the filesystem (~/.local/share/containers/storage is a xfs - If this is not the case, we get other errors)

1.3 Run rootless podman (eg. podman info)
```
podman info
Error: storage options overlay.size and overlay.inodes not supported. Filesystem does not support Project Quota: failed to mknod /home/steff/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/backingFsBlockDev.tmp: operation not permitted
```

**Attempt No 2: Use directly xfs**

2.1. Try to use directly the xfs driver insted of the overlay driver (even without size-limitation)
```
cat <<EOF >~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "xfs"
EOF
```

2.2. Clean the storage-directory and run podman info
```
rm ~/.local/share/containers/storage/* -rf 
podman info
ERRO[0000] Failed to GetDriver graph xfs /home/steff/.local/share/containers/storage
Error: failed to GetDriver graph xfs /home/steff/.local/share/containers/storage: driver not supported
```

**Describe the results you received:**
I also tried other constellations with btrfs, However none of the rootless variations with using the size-options seems to be working.
The error-message in this case however clarifies, that this only will work with xfs.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Either that it is documented that this won't work (or a error-message telling me this) or a container being created.
Furthermore I expected that the xfs driver (without size-limitation) would work in rootless mode.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Issue is reproducible

**Output of `podman version`:**
```

Version:      4.3.0

```

Yes. I tried it with both the packaged version 4.3.0 and with the even newer revision afc84151

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Physical x86_64 linux with 6.0.6-arch1-1Kernel

comments.body: @rhatdan Is this omitted because rootless cannot support it?

Rootless users are not allowed to create device nodes.
 mknod /home/steff/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/backingFsBlockDev.tmp:  operation not permitted
@giuseppe any way to work around this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe Friendly ping.
I am not too familiar with xfs quotas but I don't think that can work for unprivileged users.  The projid is not namespaced, so how to prevent it doesn't use ids used by root?
@giuseppe can btrfs make a difference? or limiting disk size for rootless containers is not possible attow
I've not tried with btrfs, and how quotas work there

```
[sleeper@ip-10-1-85-228 ~]$ podman info | grep graphDriverName
  graphDriverName: btrfs
[sleeper@ip-10-1-85-228 ~]$ podman run --rm -it --storage-opt size=10G alpine df -h
Error: unknown option size
```
but size should be supported https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/main/pkg/config/config.go#L83


EDIT:
```
[sleeper@ip-10-1-85-228 ~]$ cat  ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "btrfs"
[storage.options]
  [storage.options.btrfs]
  min_space = ""
  size = "10g"
```
```
[sleeper@ip-10-1-85-228 ~]$  podman run --rm -it  alpine df -h
Error: unknown option btrfs.size
[sleeper@ip-10-1-85-228 ~]$ sudo  podman run --rm -it  alpine df -h
Filesystem                Size      Used Available Use% Mounted on
overlay                  10.0G      8.0K     10.0G   0% /
tmpfs                    64.0M         0     64.0M   0% /dev
tmpfs                    12.4G    796.0K     12.4G   0% /run/secrets
tmpfs                    12.4G    796.0K     12.4G   0% /etc/hostname
tmpfs                    12.4G    796.0K     12.4G   0% /etc/resolv.conf
tmpfs                    12.4G    796.0K     12.4G   0% /etc/hosts
shm                      62.5M         0     62.5M   0% /dev/shm
tmpfs                    12.4G    796.0K     12.4G   0% /run/.containerenv
devtmpfs                  4.0M         0      4.0M   0% /proc/kcore
devtmpfs                  4.0M         0      4.0M   0% /proc/keys
devtmpfs                  4.0M         0      4.0M   0% /proc/latency_stats
devtmpfs                  4.0M         0      4.0M   0% /proc/timer_list
```

seems that btrfs is no go also. 


@rhatdan maybe some kind of explicit statement here https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/rootless.md would make life easier for adopters.
@giuseppe any chance "at least" to limit bytes written to disk via cgroups ? Thanks
could you take a look at the io controller `--device-write-iops`?

@giuseppe hm, thanks.

Would be doable to have some privileged service to trap syscalls from mknod for allowing creating "safe" file paths or is it total nonsense? What is the recommendation for nonworking `--storage-opt size=` arg for rootless containers?

technically that could be achieved with seccomp notifications, we have a prototype in crun and in conmon for that, but it is quite complicated so it didn't become a supported feature (most difficulty is that the seccomp profile must be configured in a way that some syscalls must be trapped, and also allow a configuration/code on how the syscall must be handled).

For the second question I am not sure there is really a way to achieve it
But the `--storage-opt` arg is omitted in API calls, right? So it won't work with third-party solutions like https://github.com/kinvolk/seccompagent/ and https://seitan.rocks/seitan/ , right?
That should not matter.  I think we need an easier way to handle these integrations, setting up `--storage-opt` manually is too complicated

@giuseppe  is possible to enable --storage-opt for remote connections for rootless? 

the logic is there for rootful:
```
[root@ip-10-1-39-63 core]# podman -r run --rm -it --storage-opt size=20g alpine df -h
Filesystem                Size      Used Available Use% Mounted on
overlay                  20.0G      8.0K     20.0G   0% /
...
```
,but now We can trap and allow syscalls via **seitan**

CLI usage has different behaviour, I would vote for keeping it the same.
```
[user@ip-10-1-39-63 ~]$ podman run --rm -it --storage-opt size=20g alpine df -h
Error: storage options overlay.size and overlay.inodes not supported. Filesystem does not support Project Quota: failed to mknod /var/mnt/ebs/graphroot/overlay/backingFsBlockDev.tmp: operation not permitte
```
```
[root@ip-10-1-39-63 core]# podman --url=unix:/run/user/1001/podman/podman.sock -r  run --rm -it --storage-opt size=20g a
lpine df -h
Filesystem                Size      Used Available Use% Mounted on
overlay                1023.5G      7.2G   1016.3G   1% /
...
```
```
[user@ip-10-1-39-63 ~]$ podman -r run --rm -it --storage-opt size=20g alpine df -h
Filesystem                Size      Used Available Use% Mounted on
overlay                1023.5G      7.2G   1016.3G   1% /
...
```

Thanks
@giuseppe is not possible to use pivot_root to mount rootfs to tmpfs ?
using tmpfs for the container rootfs should work, do you see any error with it?

Podman
```
podman run --rm -it  --mount type=tmpfs,tmpfs-size=512M,destination=/ alpine
Error: OCI runtime error: crun: mount `tmpfs` to ``: Invalid argument
```
Docker:
```
docker run --rm -it   --mount type=tmpfs,tmpfs-size=512M,destination=/ alpine
docker: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "tmpfs": invalid specification: destination can't be '/'.
```
@giuseppe or is there any other option on how to globally set disk size for all containers in rootless ?
you need to do something like `podman --root /tmp/foo` that will ensure tmpfs for the containers storage.
That does not work with API, can We get support for --storage-opt size in rootless?

this issue was closed because there is not a generic way for rootless to use quota.  The tmpfs approach could work for your use case but it is not a generic solution.

I don't know if it is possible with the btrfs backend, feel free to work on it and open a PR if you'd like.

BTRFS does not work either, see my previous comment https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16424#issuecomment-1508918989

@giuseppe still not sure how to to use tmpfs with enabled quota for rootfs, can You provide me with an example, or/and if is possible to set this on podman level in storage.conf for example. Thanks

@lukasmrtvy what I was suggesting is that you can use a tmpfs for the graphroot, e.g.

```
podman --root /tmp/foo run --rm alpine true
```

or you can manually set the mount for the container and use `--rootfs` on it:

```
podman run --rm --rootfs /tmp/foo/container true
```

but I don't see how this will bring you closer to quota support for rootless unless you manually set up a different tmpfs mount for each container and consider the tmpfs size as the container quota itself.

We can keep the discussion here but I am closing the issue again because it is unclear what we should do from Podman to enable this use case, AFAICS the kernel doesn't allow it.

@cmaiolino will it be possible to use a mechanism similar to projid for tmpfs quotas in the kernel?  Would it be possible to use it from a tmpfs mounted in a user namespace?

> We can keep the discussion here but I am closing the issue again because it is unclear what we should do from Podman to enable this use case, AFAICS the kernel doesn't allow it.
> 
> @cmaiolino will it be possible to use a mechanism similar to projid for tmpfs quotas in the kernel? Would it be possible to use it from a tmpfs mounted in a user namespace?

@giuseppe I'm working on implementing quotas for tmpfs, the first goal is to have it so that regular users can't abuse system's memory, I plan to add project quotas and namespace support for it once we get the main thing upstream. But it's indeed something that should be doable in the future
